result: 在DAVIS、DAVSOD等数据集上取得竞争性性能，单帧处理速度达0.01秒，无需复杂序列模块即可保持高效率。
conclusion: 该方法通过时空知识蒸馏有效平衡了检测精度与计算效率，为视频显著性检测提供了轻量化解决方案。
search_text: Fast Video Salient Object Detection via Spatiotemporal Knowledge Distillation 通过时空知识蒸馏的快速视频显着目标检测 Since the wide employment of deep learning frameworks in video salient object detection, the accuracy of the recent approaches has made stunning progress. These approaches mainly adopt the sequential modules, based on optical flow or recurrent neural network (RNN), to learn robust spatiotemporal features. These modules are effective but significantly increase the computational burden of the corresponding deep models. In this paper, to simplify the network and maintain the accuracy, we present a lightweight network tailored for video salient object detection through the spatiotemporal knowledge distillation. Specifically, in the spatial aspect, we combine a saliency guidance feature embedding structure and spatial knowledge distillation to refine the spatial features. In the temporal aspect, we propose a temporal knowledge distillation strategy, which allows the network to learn the robust temporal features through the infer-frame feature encoding and distilling information from adjacent frames. The experiments on widely used video datasets (e.g., DAVIS, DAVSOD, SegTrack-V2) prove that our approach achieves competitive performance. Furthermore, without the employment of the complex sequential modules, the proposed network can obtain high efficiency with 0.01s per frame. 由于深度学习框架在视频显着目标检测中的广泛应用，最近方法的准确性取得了惊人的进步。这些方法主要采用基于光流或循环神经网络（RNN）的顺序模块来学习鲁棒的时空特征。这些模块是有效的，但显着增加了相应深度模型的计算负担。在本文中，为了简化网络并保持准确性，我们提出了一种通过时空知识蒸馏为视频显着目标检测量身定制的轻量级网络。具体来说，在空间方面，我们结合显着性引导特征嵌入结构和空间知识蒸馏来细化空间特征。在时间方面，我们提出了一种时间知识蒸馏策略，该策略允许网络通过推断帧特征编码和从相邻帧中提取信息来学习鲁棒的时间特征。在广泛使用的视频数据集（例如 DAVIS、DAVSOD、SegTrack-V2）上的实验证明我们的方法实现了有竞争力的性能。此外，在不使用复杂的顺序模块的情况下，所提出的网络可以获得每帧 0.01s 的高效率。 本文提出一种基于时空知识蒸馏的轻量级网络，用于实现高效视频显著性目标检测。 Yi Tang,Yuanman Li,Wenbin Zou cs.CV

authors_text: Victor L. F. Souza,Adriano L. I. Oliveira,Rafael M. O. Cruz,Robert Sabourin
title: An Investigation of Feature Selection and Transfer Learning for Writer-Independent Offline Handwritten Signature Verification
title_zh: 独立于作者的离线手写签名验证的特征选择和迁移学习研究
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2004.03373
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2004.03373
abstract: SigNet is a state of the art model for feature representation used for handwritten signature verification (HSV). This representation is based on a Deep Convolutional Neural Network (DCNN) and contains 2048 dimensions. When transposed to a dissimilarity space generated by the dichotomy transformation (DT), related to the writer-independent (WI) approach, these features may include redundant information. This paper investigates the presence of overfitting when using Binary Particle Swarm Optimization (BPSO) to perform the feature selection in a wrapper mode. We proposed a method based on a global validation strategy with an external archive to control overfitting during the search for the most discriminant representation. Moreover, an investigation is also carried out to evaluate the use of the selected features in a transfer learning context. The analysis is carried out on a writer-independent approach on the CEDAR, MCYT and GPDS datasets. The experimental results showed the presence of overfitting when no validation is used during the optimization process and the improvement when the global validation strategy with an external archive is used. Also, the space generated after feature selection can be used in a transfer learning context.
summary: SigNet is a state of the art model for feature representation used for handwritten signature verification (HSV). This representation is based on a Deep Convolutional Neural Network (DCNN) and contains 2048 dimensions. When transposed to a dissimilarity space generated by the dichotomy transformation (DT), related to the writer-independent (WI) approach, these features may include redundant information. This paper investigates the presence of overfitting when using Binary Particle Swarm Optimization (BPSO) to perform the feature selection in a wrapper mode. We proposed a method based on a global validation strategy with an external archive to control overfitting during the search for the most discriminant representation. Moreover, an investigation is also carried out to evaluate the use of the selected features in a transfer learning context. The analysis is carried out on a writer-independent approach on the CEDAR, MCYT and GPDS datasets. The experimental results showed the presence of overfitting when no validation is used during the optimization process and the improvement when the global validation strategy with an external archive is used. Also, the space generated after feature selection can be used in a transfer learning context.
abstract_zh: SigNet 是用于手写签名验证 (HSV) 的最先进的特征表示模型。该表示基于深度卷积神经网络 (DCNN)，包含 2048 个维度。当转置到由二分变换（DT）生成的与独立于作者（WI）方法相关的相异空间时，这些特征可能包括冗余信息。本文研究了使用二进制粒子群优化 (BPSO) 在包装器模式下执行特征选择时是否存在过度拟合。我们提出了一种基于全局验证策略和外部存档的方法，以在搜索最具辨别力的表示过程中控制过度拟合。此外，还进行了一项调查来评估所选特征在迁移学习环境中的使用。该分析是在 CEDAR、MCYT 和 GPDS 数据集上采用独立于作者的方法进行的。实验结果表明，在优化过程中不使用验证时会出现过度拟合，而使用具有外部存档的全局验证策略时会有所改善。此外，特征选择后生成的空间可以用于迁移学习环境。

AI.tldr: 本论文研究了非作家依赖的离线手写签名验证中特征选择与迁移学习的应用，旨在解决特征冗余与过拟合问题。
AI.motivation: 现有特征表示方法（如SigNet）在非作家依赖场景下可能包含冗余信息，导致过拟合，影响签名验证的泛化能力。
AI.method: 采用二进制粒子群优化（BPSO）进行特征选择，结合全局验证策略与外部存档控制过拟合，并在迁移学习框架中验证所选特征的有效性。
AI.result: 实验表明，未使用验证策略时存在过拟合问题，而全局验证策略显著提升性能；特征选择后的空间可有效支持迁移学习。
AI.conclusion: 提出的特征选择方法有效缓解过拟合，验证了迁移学习在非作家依赖签名验证中的潜力，为后续研究提供新方向。

authors_text: Jovita Lukasik,David Friede,Heiner Stuckenschmidt,Margret Keuper
title: Neural Architecture Performance Prediction Using Graph Neural Networks
title_zh: 使用图神经网络进行神经架构性能预测
comment: camera ready version for DAGM GCPR 2020. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:1912.05317
comments: camera ready version for DAGM GCPR 2020. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:1912.05317
abstract: In computer vision research, the process of automating architecture engineering, Neural Architecture Search (NAS), has gained substantial interest. Due to the high computational costs, most recent approaches to NAS as well as the few available benchmarks only provide limited search spaces. In this paper we propose a surrogate model for neural architecture performance prediction built upon Graph Neural Networks (GNN). We demonstrate the effectiveness of this surrogate model on neural architecture performance prediction for structurally unknown architectures (i.e. zero shot prediction) by evaluating the GNN on several experiments on the NAS-Bench-101 dataset.
summary: In computer vision research, the process of automating architecture engineering, Neural Architecture Search (NAS), has gained substantial interest. Due to the high computational costs, most recent approaches to NAS as well as the few available benchmarks only provide limited search spaces. In this paper we propose a surrogate model for neural architecture performance prediction built upon Graph Neural Networks (GNN). We demonstrate the effectiveness of this surrogate model on neural architecture performance prediction for structurally unknown architectures (i.e. zero shot prediction) by evaluating the GNN on several experiments on the NAS-Bench-101 dataset.
abstract_zh: 在计算机视觉研究中，自动化架构工程过程、神经架构搜索（NAS）引起了人们的极大兴趣。由于计算成本较高，最新的 NAS 方法以及少数可用的基准测试仅提供有限的搜索空间。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于图神经网络（GNN）的神经架构性能预测的替代模型。我们通过在 NAS-Bench-101 数据集上的多个实验中评估 GNN，证明了该替代模型对结构未知架构（即零样本预测）的神经架构性能预测的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于图神经网络（GNN）的替代模型，用于预测神经架构性能，尤其针对结构未知的零样本预测场景。
AI.motivation: 现有神经架构搜索（NAS）方法因计算成本高且搜索空间有限，难以高效评估新架构性能，需更通用的预测模型。
AI.method: 构建图神经网络作为代理模型，将神经网络架构表示为图结构，利用GNN学习架构与性能间的映射关系，并在NAS-Bench-101数据集验证。
AI.result: 实验表明该模型能有效预测结构未知架构的性能，优于现有基准方法，在零样本预测任务中表现突出。
AI.conclusion: GNN代理模型为NAS提供了高效性能预测方案，可推广至未见过的架构设计，推动自动化架构搜索的发展。

authors_text: Thao Minh Le,Vuong Le,Svetha Venkatesh,Truyen Tran
title: Hierarchical Conditional Relation Networks for Multimodal Video Question Answering
title_zh: 用于多模态视频问答的分层条件关系网络
comment: Major extension of our CVPR'20 paper to handle long video with text. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2002.10698
comments: Major extension of our CVPR'20 paper to handle long video with text. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2002.10698
abstract: Video QA challenges modelers in multiple fronts. Modeling video necessitates building not only spatio-temporal models for the dynamic visual channel but also multimodal structures for associated information channels such as subtitles or audio. Video QA adds at least two more layers of complexity - selecting relevant content for each channel in the context of the linguistic query, and composing spatio-temporal concepts and relations in response to the query. To address these requirements, we start with two insights: (a) content selection and relation construction can be jointly encapsulated into a conditional computational structure, and (b) video-length structures can be composed hierarchically. For (a) this paper introduces a general-reusable neural unit dubbed Conditional Relation Network (CRN) taking as input a set of tensorial objects and translating into a new set of objects that encode relations of the inputs. The generic design of CRN helps ease the common complex model building process of Video QA by simple block stacking with flexibility in accommodating input modalities and conditioning features across both different domains. As a result, we realize insight (b) by introducing Hierarchical Conditional Relation Networks (HCRN) for Video QA. The HCRN primarily aims at exploiting intrinsic properties of the visual content of a video and its accompanying channels in terms of compositionality, hierarchy, and near and far-term relation. HCRN is then applied for Video QA in two forms, short-form where answers are reasoned solely from the visual content, and long-form where associated information, such as subtitles, presented. Our rigorous evaluations show consistent improvements over SOTAs on well-studied benchmarks including large-scale real-world datasets such as TGIF-QA and TVQA, demonstrating the strong capabilities of our CRN unit and the HCRN for complex domains such as Video QA.
summary: Video QA challenges modelers in multiple fronts. Modeling video necessitates building not only spatio-temporal models for the dynamic visual channel but also multimodal structures for associated information channels such as subtitles or audio. Video QA adds at least two more layers of complexity - selecting relevant content for each channel in the context of the linguistic query, and composing spatio-temporal concepts and relations in response to the query. To address these requirements, we start with two insights: (a) content selection and relation construction can be jointly encapsulated into a conditional computational structure, and (b) video-length structures can be composed hierarchically. For (a) this paper introduces a general-reusable neural unit dubbed Conditional Relation Network (CRN) taking as input a set of tensorial objects and translating into a new set of objects that encode relations of the inputs. The generic design of CRN helps ease the common complex model building process of Video QA by simple block stacking with flexibility in accommodating input modalities and conditioning features across both different domains. As a result, we realize insight (b) by introducing Hierarchical Conditional Relation Networks (HCRN) for Video QA. The HCRN primarily aims at exploiting intrinsic properties of the visual content of a video and its accompanying channels in terms of compositionality, hierarchy, and near and far-term relation. HCRN is then applied for Video QA in two forms, short-form where answers are reasoned solely from the visual content, and long-form where associated information, such as subtitles, presented. Our rigorous evaluations show consistent improvements over SOTAs on well-studied benchmarks including large-scale real-world datasets such as TGIF-QA and TVQA, demonstrating the strong capabilities of our CRN unit and the HCRN for complex domains such as Video QA.
abstract_zh: 视频质量检查在多个方面对建模者提出了挑战。视频建模不仅需要构建动态视觉通道的时空模型，还需要构建相关信息通道（例如字幕或音频）的多模态结构。视频 QA 至少增加了两层复杂性 - 在语言查询的上下文中为每个通道选择相关内容，并根据查询构建时空概念和关系。为了满足这些要求，我们从两个见解开始：（a）内容选择和关系构建可以共同封装到条件计算结构中，（b）视频长度结构可以分层组成。对于（a），本文引入了一种称为条件关系网络（CRN）的通用可重用神经单元，将一组张量对象作为输入，并转换为一组对输入关系进行编码的新对象。 CRN 的通用设计通过简单的块堆叠来帮助简化视频 QA 的常见复杂模型构建过程，并且可以灵活地适应两个不同领域的输入模式和条件特征。因此，我们通过引入用于视频 QA 的分层条件关系网络（HCRN）来实现洞察（b）。 HCRN 主要旨在利用视频视觉内容及其伴随通道在组成性、层次结构以及近远关系方面的内在属性。然后，HCRN 以两种形式应用于视频 QA，即仅根据视觉内容推理答案的短格式，以及呈现相关信息（例如字幕）的长格式。我们严格的评估表明，在经过充分研究的基准（包括 TGIF-QA 和 TVQA 等大规模现实世界数据集）上，SOTA 取得了持续改进，展示了我们的 CRN 单元和 HCRN 对于视频 QA 等复杂领域的强大能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种分层条件关系网络（HCRN），用于解决多模态视频问答任务，通过整合视觉内容和关联信息（如字幕）来提升问答性能。
AI.motivation: 视频问答需要同时处理时空视觉信息和多模态关联信息，并面临内容选择与关系构建的复杂性，传统方法难以高效建模这些层次化结构。
AI.method: 设计通用的条件关系网络（CRN）模块，通过张量输入编码对象间关系，并基于CRN构建分层结构HCRN，分别处理短形式（仅视觉）和长形式（含字幕等信息）的问答任务。
AI.result: 在TGIF-QA和TVQA等基准数据集上，HCRN在多项指标上优于现有最先进方法，验证了其对复杂多模态视频问答的有效性。
AI.conclusion: HCRN通过分层条件关系建模有效解决了视频问答中的多模态信息整合难题，为复杂场景下的问答任务提供了灵活且强大的解决方案。

search_text: Hierarchical Conditional Relation Networks for Multimodal Video Question Answering 用于多模态视频问答的分层条件关系网络 Video QA challenges modelers in multiple fronts. Modeling video necessitates building not only spatio-temporal models for the dynamic visual channel but also multimodal structures for associated information channels such as subtitles or audio. Video QA adds at least two more layers of complexity - selecting relevant content for each channel in the context of the linguistic query, and composing spatio-temporal concepts and relations in response to the query. To address these requirements, we start with two insights: (a) content selection and relation construction can be jointly encapsulated into a conditional computational structure, and (b) video-length structures can be composed hierarchically. For (a) this paper introduces a general-reusable neural unit dubbed Conditional Relation Network (CRN) taking as input a set of tensorial objects and translating into a new set of objects that encode relations of the inputs. The generic design of CRN helps ease the common complex model building process of Video QA by simple block stacking with flexibility in accommodating input modalities and conditioning features across both different domains. As a result, we realize insight (b) by introducing Hierarchical Conditional Relation Networks (HCRN) for Video QA. The HCRN primarily aims at exploiting intrinsic properties of the visual content of a video and its accompanying channels in terms of compositionality, hierarchy, and near and far-term relation. HCRN is then applied for Video QA in two forms, short-form where answers are reasoned solely from the visual content, and long-form where associated information, such as subtitles, presented. Our rigorous evaluations show consistent improvements over SOTAs on well-studied benchmarks including large-scale real-world datasets such as TGIF-QA and TVQA, demonstrating the strong capabilities of our CRN unit and the HCRN for complex domains such as Video QA. 视频质量检查在多个方面对建模者提出了挑战。视频建模不仅需要构建动态视觉通道的时空模型，还需要构建相关信息通道（例如字幕或音频）的多模态结构。视频 QA 至少增加了两层复杂性 - 在语言查询的上下文中为每个通道选择相关内容，并根据查询构建时空概念和关系。为了满足这些要求，我们从两个见解开始：（a）内容选择和关系构建可以共同封装到条件计算结构中，（b）视频长度结构可以分层组成。对于（a），本文引入了一种称为条件关系网络（CRN）的通用可重用神经单元，将一组张量对象作为输入，并转换为一组对输入关系进行编码的新对象。 CRN 的通用设计通过简单的块堆叠来帮助简化视频 QA 的常见复杂模型构建过程，并且可以灵活地适应两个不同领域的输入模式和条件特征。因此，我们通过引入用于视频 QA 的分层条件关系网络（HCRN）来实现洞察（b）。 HCRN 主要旨在利用视频视觉内容及其伴随通道在组成性、层次结构以及近远关系方面的内在属性。然后，HCRN 以两种形式应用于视频 QA，即仅根据视觉内容推理答案的短格式，以及呈现相关信息（例如字幕）的长格式。我们严格的评估表明，在经过充分研究的基准（包括 TGIF-QA 和 TVQA 等大规模现实世界数据集）上，SOTA 取得了持续改进，展示了我们的 CRN 单元和 HCRN 对于视频 QA 等复杂领域的强大能力。 本文提出了一种分层条件关系网络（HCRN），用于解决多模态视频问答任务，通过整合视觉内容和关联信息（如字幕）来提升问答性能。 Thao Minh Le,Vuong Le,Svetha Venkatesh,Truyen Tran cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Li Yuan,Shuning Chang,Xuecheng Nie,Ziyuan Huang,Yichen Zhou,Yunpeng Chen,Jiashi Feng,Shuicheng Yan
title: Towards Accurate Human Pose Estimation in Videos of Crowded Scenes
title_zh: 拥挤场景视频中准确的人体姿势估计
comment: 2nd Place in ACM Multimedia Grand Challenge: Human in Events, Track2: Crowd Pose Estimation in Complex Events. ACM Multimedia 2020. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2010.08365, arXiv:2010.10007
comments: 2nd Place in ACM Multimedia Grand Challenge: Human in Events, Track2: Crowd Pose Estimation in Complex Events. ACM Multimedia 2020. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2010.08365, arXiv:2010.10007
abstract: Video-based human pose estimation in crowded scenes is a challenging problem due to occlusion, motion blur, scale variation and viewpoint change, etc. Prior approaches always fail to deal with this problem because of (1) lacking of usage of temporal information; (2) lacking of training data in crowded scenes. In this paper, we focus on improving human pose estimation in videos of crowded scenes from the perspectives of exploiting temporal context and collecting new data. In particular, we first follow the top-down strategy to detect persons and perform single-person pose estimation for each frame. Then, we refine the frame-based pose estimation with temporal contexts deriving from the optical-flow. Specifically, for one frame, we forward the historical poses from the previous frames and backward the future poses from the subsequent frames to current frame, leading to stable and accurate human pose estimation in videos. In addition, we mine new data of similar scenes to HIE dataset from the Internet for improving the diversity of training set. In this way, our model achieves best performance on 7 out of 13 videos and 56.33 average w\_AP on test dataset of HIE challenge.
summary: Video-based human pose estimation in crowded scenes is a challenging problem due to occlusion, motion blur, scale variation and viewpoint change, etc. Prior approaches always fail to deal with this problem because of (1) lacking of usage of temporal information; (2) lacking of training data in crowded scenes. In this paper, we focus on improving human pose estimation in videos of crowded scenes from the perspectives of exploiting temporal context and collecting new data. In particular, we first follow the top-down strategy to detect persons and perform single-person pose estimation for each frame. Then, we refine the frame-based pose estimation with temporal contexts deriving from the optical-flow. Specifically, for one frame, we forward the historical poses from the previous frames and backward the future poses from the subsequent frames to current frame, leading to stable and accurate human pose estimation in videos. In addition, we mine new data of similar scenes to HIE dataset from the Internet for improving the diversity of training set. In this way, our model achieves best performance on 7 out of 13 videos and 56.33 average w\_AP on test dataset of HIE challenge.
abstract_zh: 由于遮挡、运动模糊、尺度变化和视点变化等原因，拥挤场景中基于视频的人体姿态估计是一个具有挑战性的问题。现有的方法总是无法解决这个问题，因为（1）缺乏时间信息的使用； (2)缺乏拥挤场景的训练数据。在本文中，我们重点从利用时间上下文和收集新数据的角度改进拥挤场景视频中的人体姿势估计。特别是，我们首先遵循自上而下的策略来检测人员并为每一帧执行单人姿势估计。然后，我们使用从光流导出的时间上下文来改进基于帧的姿态估计。具体来说，对于一帧，我们将前一帧的历史姿势向前推，并将后续帧的未来姿势向后推到当前帧，从而在视频中实现稳定且准确的人体姿势估计。此外，我们从互联网上挖掘与HIE数据集相似场景的新数据，以提高训练集的多样性。通过这种方式，我们的模型在 13 个视频中的 7 个上实现了最佳性能，并且在 HIE 挑战的测试数据集上获得了 56.33 的平均 w\_AP。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过利用时间信息和收集新数据，提升拥挤场景视频中人体姿态估计的准确性。
AI.motivation: 现有方法因未充分利用时间信息及缺乏拥挤场景训练数据，导致在复杂场景下姿态估计效果不佳。
AI.method: 采用自上而下策略检测并逐帧估计单人姿态，结合光流信息引入历史与未来帧姿态进行优化；同时从互联网挖掘类似场景数据以增强训练集多样性。
AI.result: 在HIE挑战测试集上，模型在13个视频中有7个表现最优，平均w_AP达56.33。
AI.conclusion: 通过时间上下文建模与数据增强，有效提升了拥挤场景视频中人体姿态估计的鲁棒性与准确性。

authors_text: Li Yuan,Shuning Chang,Ziyuan Huang,Yichen Zhou,Yunpeng Chen,Xuecheng Nie,Francis E. H. Tay,Jiashi Feng,Shuicheng Yan
title: A Simple Baseline for Pose Tracking in Videos of Crowded Scenes
title_zh: 拥挤场景视频中姿势跟踪的简单基线
comment: 2nd Place in ACM Multimedia Grand Challenge: Human in Events, Track3: Crowd Pose Tracking in Complex Events. ACM Multimedia 2020. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2010.08365, arXiv:2010.10008
comments: 2nd Place in ACM Multimedia Grand Challenge: Human in Events, Track3: Crowd Pose Tracking in Complex Events. ACM Multimedia 2020. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2010.08365, arXiv:2010.10008
abstract: This paper presents our solution to ACM MM challenge: Large-scale Human-centric Video Analysis in Complex Events\cite{lin2020human}; specifically, here we focus on Track3: Crowd Pose Tracking in Complex Events. Remarkable progress has been made in multi-pose training in recent years. However, how to track the human pose in crowded and complex environments has not been well addressed. We formulate the problem as several subproblems to be solved. First, we use a multi-object tracking method to assign human ID to each bounding box generated by the detection model. After that, a pose is generated to each bounding box with ID. At last, optical flow is used to take advantage of the temporal information in the videos and generate the final pose tracking result.
summary: This paper presents our solution to ACM MM challenge: Large-scale Human-centric Video Analysis in Complex Events\cite{lin2020human}; specifically, here we focus on Track3: Crowd Pose Tracking in Complex Events. Remarkable progress has been made in multi-pose training in recent years. However, how to track the human pose in crowded and complex environments has not been well addressed. We formulate the problem as several subproblems to be solved. First, we use a multi-object tracking method to assign human ID to each bounding box generated by the detection model. After that, a pose is generated to each bounding box with ID. At last, optical flow is used to take advantage of the temporal information in the videos and generate the final pose tracking result.
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了我们针对 ACM MM 挑战的解决方案：复杂事件中的大规模以人为中心的视频分析\cite{lin2020 human}；具体来说，这里我们重点关注 Track3：复杂事件中的人群姿势跟踪。近年来，多姿势训练取得了显着的进展。然而，如何在拥挤和复杂的环境中跟踪人体姿势尚未得到很好的解决。我们将问题表述为几个需要解决的子问题。首先，我们使用多目标跟踪方法将人体 ID 分配给检测模型生成的每个边界框。之后，为每个带有 ID 的边界框生成一个姿势。最后，使用光流来利用视频中的时间信息并生成最终的姿势跟踪结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种针对拥挤场景中视频人体姿态跟踪的简单基线方法，解决ACM MM挑战赛中的Crowd Pose Tracking问题。
AI.motivation: 尽管多姿态训练取得进展，但如何在拥挤复杂的环境中跟踪人体姿态仍缺乏有效解决方案。
AI.method: 将问题分解为三个子问题：1) 使用多目标跟踪为检测框分配ID；2) 为每个带ID的检测框生成姿态；3) 利用光流法利用视频时序信息生成最终跟踪结果。
AI.result: 该方法在ACM MM挑战赛Track3中取得有效结果，验证了其在复杂场景下的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 通过分阶段处理和时序信息利用，本文为拥挤场景的人体姿态跟踪提供了可扩展的解决方案，未来可进一步优化跟踪精度与鲁棒性。

search_text: A Simple Baseline for Pose Tracking in Videos of Crowded Scenes 拥挤场景视频中姿势跟踪的简单基线 This paper presents our solution to ACM MM challenge: Large-scale Human-centric Video Analysis in Complex Events\cite{lin2020human}; specifically, here we focus on Track3: Crowd Pose Tracking in Complex Events. Remarkable progress has been made in multi-pose training in recent years. However, how to track the human pose in crowded and complex environments has not been well addressed. We formulate the problem as several subproblems to be solved. First, we use a multi-object tracking method to assign human ID to each bounding box generated by the detection model. After that, a pose is generated to each bounding box with ID. At last, optical flow is used to take advantage of the temporal information in the videos and generate the final pose tracking result. 本文介绍了我们针对 ACM MM 挑战的解决方案：复杂事件中的大规模以人为中心的视频分析\cite{lin2020 human}；具体来说，这里我们重点关注 Track3：复杂事件中的人群姿势跟踪。近年来，多姿势训练取得了显着的进展。然而，如何在拥挤和复杂的环境中跟踪人体姿势尚未得到很好的解决。我们将问题表述为几个需要解决的子问题。首先，我们使用多目标跟踪方法将人体 ID 分配给检测模型生成的每个边界框。之后，为每个带有 ID 的边界框生成一个姿势。最后，使用光流来利用视频中的时间信息并生成最终的姿势跟踪结果。 本文提出了一种针对拥挤场景中视频人体姿态跟踪的简单基线方法，解决ACM MM挑战赛中的Crowd Pose Tracking问题。 Li Yuan,Shuning Chang,Ziyuan Huang,Yichen Zhou,Yunpeng Chen,Xuecheng Nie,Francis E. authors_text: Long Chen,Feixiang Zhou,Shengke Wang,Junyu Dong,Ning Li,Haiping Ma,Xin Wang,Huiyu Zhou
title: SWIPENET: Object detection in noisy underwater images
title_zh: SWIPENET：噪声水下图像中的目标检测
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2005.11552
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2005.11552
abstract: In recent years, deep learning based object detection methods have achieved promising performance in controlled environments. However, these methods lack sufficient capabilities to handle underwater object detection due to these challenges: (1) images in the underwater datasets and real applications are blurry whilst accompanying severe noise that confuses the detectors and (2) objects in real applications are usually small. In this paper, we propose a novel Sample-WeIghted hyPEr Network (SWIPENET), and a robust training paradigm named Curriculum Multi-Class Adaboost (CMA), to address these two problems at the same time. Firstly, the backbone of SWIPENET produces multiple high resolution and semantic-rich Hyper Feature Maps, which significantly improve small object detection. Secondly, a novel sample-weighted detection loss function is designed for SWIPENET, which focuses on learning high weight samples and ignore learning low weight samples. Moreover, inspired by the human education process that drives the learning from easy to hard concepts, we here propose the CMA training paradigm that first trains a clean detector which is free from the influence of noisy data. Then, based on the clean detector, multiple detectors focusing on learning diverse noisy data are trained and incorporated into a unified deep ensemble of strong noise immunity. Experiments on two underwater robot picking contest datasets (URPC2017 and URPC2018) show that the proposed SWIPENET+CMA framework achieves better accuracy in object detection against several state-of-the-art approaches.
summary: In recent years, deep learning based object detection methods have achieved promising performance in controlled environments. However, these methods lack sufficient capabilities to handle underwater object detection due to these challenges: (1) images in the underwater datasets and real applications are blurry whilst accompanying severe noise that confuses the detectors and (2) objects in real applications are usually small. In this paper, we propose a novel Sample-WeIghted hyPEr Network (SWIPENET), and a robust training paradigm named Curriculum Multi-Class Adaboost (CMA), to address these two problems at the same time. Firstly, the backbone of SWIPENET produces multiple high resolution and semantic-rich Hyper Feature Maps, which significantly improve small object detection. Secondly, a novel sample-weighted detection loss function is designed for SWIPENET, which focuses on learning high weight samples and ignore learning low weight samples. Moreover, inspired by the human education process that drives the learning from easy to hard concepts, we here propose the CMA training paradigm that first trains a clean detector which is free from the influence of noisy data. Then, based on the clean detector, multiple detectors focusing on learning diverse noisy data are trained and incorporated into a unified deep ensemble of strong noise immunity. Experiments on two underwater robot picking contest datasets (URPC2017 and URPC2018) show that the proposed SWIPENET+CMA framework achieves better accuracy in object detection against several state-of-the-art approaches.
abstract_zh: 近年来，基于深度学习的目标检测方法在受控环境中取得了可喜的性能。然而，由于以下挑战，这些方法缺乏足够的能力来处理水下物体检测：（1）水下数据集中和实际应用中的图像模糊，同时伴随着严重的噪声，使探测器感到困惑；（2）实际应用中的物体通常很小。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的样本加权超网络（SWIPENET）和一种名为课程多类 Adaboost（CMA）的稳健训练范例，以同时解决这两个问题。首先，SWIPENET 的主干网产生多个高分辨率且语义丰富的超特征图，这显着改善了小物体检测。其次，为SWIPENET设计了一种新颖的样本加权检测损失函数，该函数专注于学习高权重样本，而忽略学习低权重样本。此外，受人类教育过程从易到难的概念学习的启发，我们在这里提出了 CMA 训练范式，首先训练一个不受噪声数据影响的干净检测器。然后，基于干净的检测器，对专注于学习不同噪声数据的多个检测器进行训练，并将其合并到具有强抗噪性的统一深度集成中。在两个水下机器人拾取竞赛数据集（URPC2017 和 URPC2018）上的实验表明，所提出的 SWIPENET+CMA 框架在目标检测方面比几种最先进的方法实现了更好的准确性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SWIPENET和CMA训练范式，解决水下图像中因噪声和小目标导致的物体检测难题。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习检测方法难以处理水下图像的模糊噪声和小目标问题，需设计针对性框架提升检测性能。
AI.method: 设计SWIPENET网络生成高分辨率语义特征图，并采用样本加权损失函数；提出CMA训练范式，通过逐步训练清洁检测器和多噪声检测器构建强鲁棒性集成模型。
AI.result: 在URPC2017和URPC2018数据集上，SWIPENET+CMA框架优于多种先进方法，检测精度显著提升。
AI.conclusion: SWIPENET结合CMA训练策略有效解决了水下噪声干扰和小目标检测问题，验证了方法的优越性。

search_text: SWIPENET: Object detection in noisy underwater images SWIPENET：噪声水下图像中的目标检测 In recent years, deep learning based object detection methods have achieved promising performance in controlled environments. However, these methods lack sufficient capabilities to handle underwater object detection due to these challenges: (1) images in the underwater datasets and real applications are blurry whilst accompanying severe noise that confuses the detectors and (2) objects in real applications are usually small. In this paper, we propose a novel Sample-WeIghted hyPEr Network (SWIPENET), and a robust training paradigm named Curriculum Multi-Class Adaboost (CMA), to address these two problems at the same time. Firstly, the backbone of SWIPENET produces multiple high resolution and semantic-rich Hyper Feature Maps, which significantly improve small object detection. Secondly, a novel sample-weighted detection loss function is designed for SWIPENET, which focuses on learning high weight samples and ignore learning low weight samples. Moreover, inspired by the human education process that drives the learning from easy to hard concepts, we here propose the CMA training paradigm that first trains a clean detector which is free from the influence of noisy data. Then, based on the clean detector, multiple detectors focusing on learning diverse noisy data are trained and incorporated into a unified deep ensemble of strong noise immunity. Experiments on two underwater robot picking contest datasets (URPC2017 and URPC2018) show that the proposed SWIPENET+CMA framework achieves better accuracy in object detection against several state-of-the-art approaches. 近年来，基于深度学习的目标检测方法在受控环境中取得了可喜的性能。然而，由于以下挑战，这些方法缺乏足够的能力来处理水下物体检测：（1）水下数据集中和实际应用中的图像模糊，同时伴随着严重的噪声，使探测器感到困惑；（2）实际应用中的物体通常很小。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的样本加权超网络（SWIPENET）和一种名为课程多类 Adaboost（CMA）的稳健训练范例，以同时解决这两个问题。首先，SWIPENET 的主干网产生多个高分辨率且语义丰富的超特征图，这显着改善了小物体检测。其次，为SWIPENET设计了一种新颖的样本加权检测损失函数，该函数专注于学习高权重样本，而忽略学习低权重样本。此外，受人类教育过程从易到难的概念学习的启发，我们在这里提出了 CMA 训练范式，首先训练一个不受噪声数据影响的干净检测器。然后，基于干净的检测器，对专注于学习不同噪声数据的多个检测器进行训练，并将其合并到具有强抗噪性的统一深度集成中。在两个水下机器人拾取竞赛数据集（URPC2017 和 URPC2018）上的实验表明，所提出的 SWIPENET+CMA 框架在目标检测方面比几种最先进的方法实现了更好的准确性。 本文提出SWIPENET和CMA训练范式，解决水下图像中因噪声和小目标导致的物体检测难题。 Long Chen,Feixiang Zhou,Shengke Wang,Junyu Dong,Ning Li,Haiping Ma,Xin Wang,Huiyu Zhou cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Luis Leal,Marvin Castillo,Fernando Juarez,Erick Ramirez,Mildred Aspuac,Diana Letona
title: Convolutional-LSTM for Multi-Image to Single Output Medical Prediction
title_zh: 用于多图像到单输出医学预测的卷积 LSTM
abstract: Medical head CT-scan imaging has been successfully combined with deep learning for medical diagnostics of head diseases and lesions[1]. State of the art classification models and algorithms for this task usually are based on 3d convolution layers for volumetric data on a supervised learning setting (1 input volume, 1 prediction per patient) or 2d convolution layers in a supervised setting (1 input image, 1 prediction per image). However a very common scenario in developing countries is to have the volume metadata lost due multiple reasons for example formatting conversion in images (for example .dicom to jpg), in this scenario the doctor analyses the collection of images and then emits a single diagnostic for the patient (with possibly an unfixed and variable number of images per patient) , this prevents it from being possible to use state of the art 3d models, but also is not possible to convert it to a supervised problem in a (1 image,1 diagnostic) setting because different angles or positions of the images for a single patient may not contain the disease or lesion. In this study we propose a solution for this scenario by combining 2d convolutional[2] models with sequence models which generate a prediction only after all images have been processed by the model for a given patient \(i\), this creates a multi-image to single-diagnostic setting \(y^i=f(x_1,x_2,..,x_n)\) where \(n\) may be different between patients. The experimental results demonstrate that it is possible to get a multi-image to single diagnostic model which mimics human doctor diagnostic process: evaluate the collection of patient images and then use important information in memory to decide a single diagnostic for the patient.
summary: Medical head CT-scan imaging has been successfully combined with deep learning for medical diagnostics of head diseases and lesions[1]. State of the art classification models and algorithms for this task usually are based on 3d convolution layers for volumetric data on a supervised learning setting (1 input volume, 1 prediction per patient) or 2d convolution layers in a supervised setting (1 input image, 1 prediction per image). However a very common scenario in developing countries is to have the volume metadata lost due multiple reasons for example formatting conversion in images (for example .dicom to jpg), in this scenario the doctor analyses the collection of images and then emits a single diagnostic for the patient (with possibly an unfixed and variable number of images per patient) , this prevents it from being possible to use state of the art 3d models, but also is not possible to convert it to a supervised problem in a (1 image,1 diagnostic) setting because different angles or positions of the images for a single patient may not contain the disease or lesion. In this study we propose a solution for this scenario by combining 2d convolutional[2] models with sequence models which generate a prediction only after all images have been processed by the model for a given patient \(i\), this creates a multi-image to single-diagnostic setting \(y^i=f(x_1,x_2,..,x_n)\) where \(n\) may be different between patients. The experimental results demonstrate that it is possible to get a multi-image to single diagnostic model which mimics human doctor diagnostic process: evaluate the collection of patient images and then use important information in memory to decide a single diagnostic for the patient.
abstract_zh: 医用头部CT扫描成像已成功与深度学习相结合，用于头部疾病和病变的医学诊断[1]。用于此任务的最先进的分类模型和算法通常基于监督学习设置中体积数据的 3d 卷积层（1 个输入体积，每个患者 1 个预测）或监督设置中的 2d 卷积层（1 个输入图像，每个图像 1 个预测）。然而，在发展中国家，一种非常常见的情况是由于多种原因导致卷元数据丢失，例如图像中的格式转换（例如 .dicom 到 jpg），在这种情况下，医生分析图像集合，然后为患者发出单个诊断（每个患者可能具有不固定且可变数量的图像），这阻止了使用最先进的 3D 模型，而且也不可能将其转换为（1 个图像，1 个诊断）设置中的监督问题，因为不同的角度或单个患者的图像位置可能不包含疾病或病变。在本研究中，我们提出了针对这种情况的解决方案，将 2d 卷积[2] 模型与序列模型相结合，序列模型仅在给定患者 \(i\) 的模型处理完所有图像后才生成预测，这创建了多图像到单诊断设置 \(y^i=f(x_1,x_2,..,x_n)\)，其中 \(n\) 可能因患者而异。实验结果表明，可以获得模仿人类医生诊断过程的多图像到单诊断模型：评估患者图像的集合，然后使用内存中的重要信息来决定对患者的单次诊断。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合2D卷积和LSTM的模型，用于处理多张医学图像并生成单个诊断结果，以解决发展中国家因3D数据丢失导致的医疗诊断问题。
AI.motivation: 在发展中国家，由于图像格式转换等原因，3D医学影像数据常丢失，医生需基于多张2D图像进行诊断，而现有模型无法处理此类多图像到单诊断的场景。
AI.method: 将2D卷积网络与LSTM序列模型结合，通过处理患者所有图像序列后生成最终诊断，实现多图像到单输出的映射。
AI.result: 实验表明，该模型能有效模拟医生诊断过程，在不同数量图像输入下保持诊断准确性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为3D数据缺失场景下的医疗诊断提供了可行方案，通过多图像序列建模提升了诊断可靠性。

search_text: Convolutional-LSTM for Multi-Image to Single Output Medical Prediction 用于多图像到单输出医学预测的卷积 LSTM Medical head CT-scan imaging has been successfully combined with deep learning for medical diagnostics of head diseases and lesions[1]. State of the art classification models and algorithms for this task usually are based on 3d convolution layers for volumetric data on a supervised learning setting (1 input volume, 1 prediction per patient) or 2d convolution layers in a supervised setting (1 input image, 1 prediction per image). However a very common scenario in developing countries is to have the volume metadata lost due multiple reasons for example formatting conversion in images (for example .dicom to jpg), in this scenario the doctor analyses the collection of images and then emits a single diagnostic for the patient (with possibly an unfixed and variable number of images per patient) , this prevents it from being possible to use state of the art 3d models, but also is not possible to convert it to a supervised problem in a (1 image,1 diagnostic) setting because different angles or positions of the images for a single patient may not contain the disease or lesion. In this study we propose a solution for this scenario by combining 2d convolutional[2] models with sequence models which generate a prediction only after all images have been processed by the model for a given patient \(i\), this creates a multi-image to single-diagnostic setting \(y^i=f(x_1,x_2,..,x_n)\) where \(n\) may be different between patients. The experimental results demonstrate that it is possible to get a multi-image to single diagnostic model which mimics human doctor diagnostic process: evaluate the collection of patient images and then use important information in memory to decide a single diagnostic for the patient. 医用头部CT扫描成像已成功与深度学习相结合，用于头部疾病和病变的医学诊断[1]。用于此任务的最先进的分类模型和算法通常基于监督学习设置中体积数据的 3d 卷积层（1 个输入体积，每个患者 1 个预测）或监督设置中的 2d 卷积层（1 个输入图像，每个图像 1 个预测）。然而，在发展中国家，一种非常常见的情况是由于多种原因导致卷元数据丢失，例如图像中的格式转换（例如 .dicom 到 jpg），在这种情况下，医生分析图像集合，然后为患者发出单个诊断（每个患者可能具有不固定且可变数量的图像），这阻止了使用最先进的 3D 模型，而且也不可能将其转换为（1 个图像，1 个诊断）设置中的监督问题，因为不同的角度或单个患者的图像位置可能不包含疾病或病变。在本研究中，我们提出了针对这种情况的解决方案，将 2d 卷积[2] 模型与序列模型相结合，序列模型仅在给定患者 \(i\) 的模型处理完所有图像后才生成预测，这创建了多图像到单诊断设置 \(y^i=f(x_1,x_2,..,x_n)\)，其中 \(n\) 可能因患者而异。实验结果表明，可以获得模仿人类医生诊断过程的多图像到单诊断模型：评估患者图像的集合，然后使用内存中的重要信息来决定对患者的单次诊断。 本文提出了一种结合2D卷积和LSTM的模型，用于处理多张医学图像并生成单个诊断结果，以解决发展中国家因3D数据丢失导致的医疗诊断问题。 Luis Leal,Marvin Castillo,Fernando Juarez,Erick Ramirez,Mildred Aspuac,Diana Letona cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Hai Wang,Wei-Shi Zheng,Ling Yingbiao
title: Contextual Heterogeneous Graph Network for Human-Object Interaction Detection
title_zh: 用于人机交互检测的上下文异构图网络
abstract: Human-object interaction(HOI) detection is an important task for understanding human activity. Graph structure is appropriate to denote the HOIs in the scene. Since there is an subordination between human and object---human play subjective role and object play objective role in HOI, the relations between homogeneous entities and heterogeneous entities in the scene should also not be equally the same. However, previous graph models regard human and object as the same kind of nodes and do not consider that the messages are not equally the same between different entities. In this work, we address such a problem for HOI task by proposing a heterogeneous graph network that models humans and objects as different kinds of nodes and incorporates intra-class messages between homogeneous nodes and inter-class messages between heterogeneous nodes. In addition, a graph attention mechanism based on the intra-class context and inter-class context is exploited to improve the learning. Extensive experiments on the benchmark datasets V-COCO and HICO-DET demonstrate that the intra-class and inter-class messages are very important in HOI detection and verify the effectiveness of our method.
summary: Human-object interaction(HOI) detection is an important task for understanding human activity. Graph structure is appropriate to denote the HOIs in the scene. Since there is an subordination between human and object---human play subjective role and object play objective role in HOI, the relations between homogeneous entities and heterogeneous entities in the scene should also not be equally the same. However, previous graph models regard human and object as the same kind of nodes and do not consider that the messages are not equally the same between different entities. In this work, we address such a problem for HOI task by proposing a heterogeneous graph network that models humans and objects as different kinds of nodes and incorporates intra-class messages between homogeneous nodes and inter-class messages between heterogeneous nodes. In addition, a graph attention mechanism based on the intra-class context and inter-class context is exploited to improve the learning. Extensive experiments on the benchmark datasets V-COCO and HICO-DET demonstrate that the intra-class and inter-class messages are very important in HOI detection and verify the effectiveness of our method.
abstract_zh: 人机交互（HOI）检测是理解人类活动的一项重要任务。图结构适合表示场景中的 HOI。由于人与物体之间存在从属关系——人在HOI中扮演主观角色，物体扮演客观角色，因此场景中的同质实体和异质实体之间的关系也不应该是同等相同的。然而，以前的图模型将人和物体视为同一类节点，并没有考虑到不同实体之间的消息并不相同。在这项工作中，我们通过提出一种异构图网络来解决 HOI 任务的此类问题，该网络将人类和物体建模为不同类型的节点，并合并同质节点之间的类内消息和异构节点之间的类间消息。此外，利用基于类内上下文和类间上下文的图注意机制来改进学习。在基准数据集 V-COCO 和 HICO-DET 上进行的大量实验表明，类内和类间消息在 HOI 检测中非常重要，并验证了我们方法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种上下文异构图网络，用于解决人体-物体交互检测中人和物体节点消息传递不均衡的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统图模型将人和物体视为相同节点类型，未考虑其在交互中的主客观角色差异，导致消息传递权重不均衡，影响检测效果。
AI.method: 构建异构图网络，区分人类与物体为不同节点类型，融合同类节点内消息与异类节点间消息，并引入基于上下文的图注意力机制优化学习过程。
AI.result: 在V-COCO和HICO-DET数据集上的实验表明，所提方法显著提升了HOI检测性能，验证了同类/异类消息的重要性。
AI.conclusion: 通过建模节点异质性与上下文注意力机制，本文有效解决了HOI检测中的消息传递不平衡问题，为交互检测提供了新思路。

authors_text: Chien-Chuan Su,Ren Wang,Hung-Jin Lin,Yu-Lun Liu,Chia-Ping Chen,Yu-Lin Chang,Soo-Chang Pei
title: Explorable Tone Mapping Operators
title_zh: 可探索的色调映射运算符
abstract: Tone-mapping plays an essential role in high dynamic range (HDR) imaging. It aims to preserve visual information of HDR images in a medium with a limited dynamic range. Although many works have been proposed to provide tone-mapped results from HDR images, most of them can only perform tone-mapping in a single pre-designed way. However, the subjectivity of tone-mapping quality varies from person to person, and the preference of tone-mapping style also differs from application to application. In this paper, a learning-based multimodal tone-mapping method is proposed, which not only achieves excellent visual quality but also explores the style diversity. Based on the framework of BicycleGAN, the proposed method can provide a variety of expert-level tone-mapped results by manipulating different latent codes. Finally, we show that the proposed method performs favorably against state-of-the-art tone-mapping algorithms both quantitatively and qualitatively.
summary: Tone-mapping plays an essential role in high dynamic range (HDR) imaging. It aims to preserve visual information of HDR images in a medium with a limited dynamic range. Although many works have been proposed to provide tone-mapped results from HDR images, most of them can only perform tone-mapping in a single pre-designed way. However, the subjectivity of tone-mapping quality varies from person to person, and the preference of tone-mapping style also differs from application to application. In this paper, a learning-based multimodal tone-mapping method is proposed, which not only achieves excellent visual quality but also explores the style diversity. Based on the framework of BicycleGAN, the proposed method can provide a variety of expert-level tone-mapped results by manipulating different latent codes. Finally, we show that the proposed method performs favorably against state-of-the-art tone-mapping algorithms both quantitatively and qualitatively.
abstract_zh: 色调映射在高动态范围 (HDR) 成像中起着至关重要的作用。它的目的是在动态范围有限的介质中保留 HDR 图像的视觉信息。尽管已经提出了许多工作来提供 HDR 图像的色调映射结果，但大多数工作只能以单一预先设计的方式执行色调映射。然而，色调映射质量的主观性因人而异，并且色调映射风格的偏好也因应用而异。本文提出了一种基于学习的多模态色调映射方法，不仅实现了出色的视觉质量，而且探索了风格多样性。基于BicycleGAN的框架，所提出的方法可以通过操纵不同的潜在代码来提供各种专家级的色调映射结果。最后，我们表明所提出的方法在定量和定性方面都优于最先进的色调映射算法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度学习的多模态色调映射方法，通过操作潜在编码生成多种专家级色调映射结果，以提升高动态范围（HDR）图像的视觉质量和风格多样性。
AI.motivation: 现有色调映射方法通常采用单一预设方式，难以满足不同用户对视觉质量的主观偏好和不同应用场景的风格需求。
AI.method: 基于BicycleGAN框架，通过操作不同潜在编码实现对色调映射风格的多样化探索。
AI.result: 在定量和定性评估中，该方法均优于现有最先进的色调映射算法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效探索了色调映射的风格多样性，同时实现了优秀的视觉质量。

In this paper, a learning-based multimodal tone-mapping method is proposed, which not only achieves excellent visual quality but also explores the style diversity. Based on the framework of BicycleGAN, the proposed method can provide a variety of expert-level tone-mapped results by manipulating different latent codes. Finally, we show that the proposed method performs favorably against state-of-the-art tone-mapping algorithms both quantitatively and qualitatively. 色调映射在高动态范围 (HDR) 成像中起着至关重要的作用。它的目的是在动态范围有限的介质中保留 HDR 图像的视觉信息。尽管已经提出了许多工作来提供 HDR 图像的色调映射结果，但大多数工作只能以单一预先设计的方式执行色调映射。然而，色调映射质量的主观性因人而异，并且色调映射风格的偏好也因应用而异。本文提出了一种基于学习的多模态色调映射方法，不仅实现了出色的视觉质量，而且探索了风格多样性。基于BicycleGAN的框架，所提出的方法可以通过操纵不同的潜在代码来提供各种专家级的色调映射结果。最后，我们表明所提出的方法在定量和定性方面都优于最先进的色调映射算法。 本文提出一种基于深度学习的多模态色调映射方法，通过操作潜在编码生成多种专家级色调映射结果，以提升高动态范围（HDR）图像的视觉质量和风格多样性。 Chien-Chuan Su,Ren Wang,Hung-Jin Lin,Yu-Lun Liu,Chia-Ping Chen,Yu-Lin Chang,Soo-Chang Pei cs.CV,eess.IV

authors_text: Haohan Wang,Peiyan Zhang,Eric P. Xing
title: Word Shape Matters: Robust Machine Translation with Visual Embedding
title_zh: 词形很重要：具有视觉嵌入的强大机器翻译
abstract: Neural machine translation has achieved remarkable empirical performance over standard benchmark datasets, yet recent evidence suggests that the models can still fail easily dealing with substandard inputs such as misspelled words, To overcome this issue, we introduce a new encoding heuristic of the input symbols for character-level NLP models: it encodes the shape of each character through the images depicting the letters when printed. We name this new strategy visual embedding and it is expected to improve the robustness of NLP models because humans also process the corpus visually through printed letters, instead of machinery one-hot vectors. Empirically, our method improves models' robustness against substandard inputs, even in the test scenario where the models are tested with the noises that are beyond what is available during the training phase.
summary: Neural machine translation has achieved remarkable empirical performance over standard benchmark datasets, yet recent evidence suggests that the models can still fail easily dealing with substandard inputs such as misspelled words, To overcome this issue, we introduce a new encoding heuristic of the input symbols for character-level NLP models: it encodes the shape of each character through the images depicting the letters when printed. We name this new strategy visual embedding and it is expected to improve the robustness of NLP models because humans also process the corpus visually through printed letters, instead of machinery one-hot vectors. Empirically, our method improves models' robustness against substandard inputs, even in the test scenario where the models are tested with the noises that are beyond what is available during the training phase.
abstract_zh: 神经机器翻译在标准基准数据集上取得了显着的经验表现，但最近的证据表明，模型在处理不标准的输入（例如拼写错误的单词）时仍然很容易失败。为了克服这个问题，我们为字符级 NLP 模型引入了一种新的输入符号编码启发式：它通过打印时描述字母的图像对每个字符的形状进行编码。我们将这种新策略命名为视觉嵌入，它有望提高 NLP 模型的鲁棒性，因为人类还通过印刷字母而不是机器的单热向量来视觉处理语料库。根据经验，我们的方法提高了模型针对不合格输入的鲁棒性，即使在模型使用超出训练阶段可用噪声的测试场景中也是如此。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为视觉嵌入的新方法，通过字符的图像形状编码来增强神经机器翻译模型对次优输入的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有神经机器翻译模型在处理拼写错误等次优输入时表现不佳，而人类处理文字时依赖于字符的视觉形状信息，因此需探索更符合人类处理方式的编码方法。
AI.method: 通过将每个字符的印刷图像转换为视觉特征嵌入，替代传统的一-hot 编码，使模型能够利用字符形状信息进行更鲁棒的翻译。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法有效提升了模型对训练阶段未见的噪声输入的鲁棒性，尤其在拼写错误等场景下表现更优。
AI.conclusion: 视觉嵌入策略能显著增强模型对次优输入的处理能力，未来可进一步探索结合更多视觉信息的NLP模型设计。
